# CROS - Communal Relationship Operating System

> CROS is the operating system for human-centered organizations. Where most platforms track transactions, CROS remembers people. Where traditional AI analyzes data, NRI learns from human experience.

> For the full content of every marketing page, see: https://thecros.app/llms-full.txt

CROS replaces CRM with relationship memory, community awareness, and narrative intelligence -- helping churches, nonprofits, social enterprises, and mission-driven organizations grow without losing their humanity.

## Core Pages

- [Homepage](https://thecros.app/): Primary landing — manifesto hero, archetype signals, and conversion pathways. CROS is not another CRM. It is a living relationship system.
- [Manifesto](https://thecros.app/manifesto): The founding narrative — why CROS exists. Long-form editorial positioning that establishes the philosophy of relationship memory over transaction tracking.
- [CROS™ Feature Overview](https://thecros.app/cros): What CROS is — the relationship operating system overview. Explains Civitas, NRI, and the Latin module structure.
- [NRI™ (Neary)](https://thecros.app/nri): Narrative Relational Intelligence — how CROS uses human-first signals instead of traditional AI analytics. NRI is not artificial intelligence; it is human-first intelligence built from reflections, events, conversations, and community signals.
- [Profunda™ Origin](https://thecros.app/profunda): The founding story — how Profunda became CROS. The internal instance that inspired the platform.
- [Pricing](https://thecros.app/pricing): Tier breakdown — CROS Core (free foundation), CROS Insight (patterns & signals), CROS Story (impact narratives), CROS Bridge (CRM migration).
- [Contact](https://thecros.app/contact): Inquiry form for prospective organizations exploring CROS.
- [Security](https://thecros.app/security): Data boundaries, row-level security, privacy commitments, and privacy-first architecture.

## Feature Modules

- [Features Overview](https://thecros.app/features): Unified feature map connecting all CROS modules.
- [Signum](https://thecros.app/signum): Discovery & signal intelligence — surfaces community events, partner movements, and local pulse without manual searching.
- [Testimonium](https://thecros.app/testimonium-feature): Narrative storytelling & insight layer — transforms daily field observations into board-ready stories.
- [Communio](https://thecros.app/communio-feature): Inter-organization collaboration network — organizations share anonymized signals and form Familia groups.
- [Impulsus](https://thecros.app/impulsus): Private impact scrapbook journal — a sacred, personal space for leaders to capture observations.
- [Voluntārium](https://thecros.app/voluntarium): Volunteer management with availability tracking, role assignments, and gentle scheduling rhythms.
- [Prōvīsiō](https://thecros.app/provisio): Technology provisions & internal resource requests.
- [Relatio Campaigns](https://thecros.app/relatio-campaigns): Email campaign orchestration — relationship-centered outreach powered by contact journey context.
- [Integrations](https://thecros.app/integrations): CRM bridge — HubSpot, Salesforce, Dynamics 365 two-way sync via Relatio™.

## Roles

CROS recognizes four identity roles. Each person in a mission-driven organization sees the system through their own lens.

- [Roles Index](https://thecros.app/roles): Overview of all CROS roles — Shepherd, Companion, Visitor, Steward.
- [Shepherd](https://thecros.app/roles/shepherd): The Regional Impact Manager — those who hold the longer story and carry the mission across relationships.
- [Companion](https://thecros.app/roles/companion): The daily relationship keeper — staff who follow up, remember details, and maintain connection threads.
- [Visitor](https://thecros.app/roles/visitor): The mobile-first field worker — those who go where the people are, recording voice notes and site visits.
- [Steward](https://thecros.app/roles/steward): The operations & admin role — those who keep systems clean, data accurate, and teams equipped.

## Archetypes — A Week Inside CROS

Each organization type experiences CROS differently. These 7-day narrative stories show realistic daily rhythms.

- [Archetypes Index](https://thecros.app/archetypes): Mission archetype selector — Church, Nonprofit, Social Enterprise, Community Network, Ministry Outreach.
- [Church Week](https://thecros.app/archetypes/church-week): Monday through Sunday with a church using CROS — morning prayers, pastoral visits, Sunday reflections.
- [Nonprofit Week](https://thecros.app/archetypes/nonprofit-week): Digital inclusion nonprofit — site visits, partner engagement, board narrative.
- [Social Enterprise Week](https://thecros.app/archetypes/social-enterprise-week): Purpose-driven business tracking social impact alongside revenue relationships.
- [Community Network Week](https://thecros.app/archetypes/community-network-week): Coalition coordinating across multiple organizations and shared geography.
- [Ministry Outreach Week](https://thecros.app/archetypes/ministry-outreach-week): Parish team using CROS for pastoral care, home visits, and small groups.
- [For Companions](https://thecros.app/for-companions): Landing for Companion archetypes — Solo and Organization modes with privacy-first positioning.

## Pilgrim Pathways — Identity-First Onboarding

- [Shepherd Pathway](https://thecros.app/path/shepherd): "You hold the longer story." For mission leaders and executive directors.
- [Companion Pathway](https://thecros.app/path/companion): "You keep the thread." For field staff and relationship keepers.
- [Steward Pathway](https://thecros.app/path/steward): "You keep the systems running." For operations and admin leaders.
- [Visitor Pathway](https://thecros.app/path/visitor): "You go where the people are." For mobile field workers.

## Callings — Mission Themes

- [Home Visitation](https://thecros.app/calling/home-visitation): The ministry of presence — home visitation software and field visit tools.
- [Parish Outreach](https://thecros.app/calling/parish-outreach): Reaching beyond the walls — church community engagement.
- [Community Support](https://thecros.app/calling/community-support): Sustaining the daily work of caring — community support software.

## Comparison & Proof

- [Compare Index](https://thecros.app/compare): CROS vs traditional CRMs — positions CROS as a relationship system, not a sales pipeline.
- [Proof](https://thecros.app/proof): Evidence and social proof — testimonials, usage signals, and trust indicators.
- [Our Story](https://thecros.app/case-study-humanity): Universal humanity case study — the founding story through the lens of community impact.
- [Relational Fundraising](https://thecros.app/fundraising-without-a-donor-crm): Why fundraising works better without a donor CRM.
- [See People](https://thecros.app/see-people): "What if your system saw people, not records?" Manifesto-style conversion page.
- [Imagine This](https://thecros.app/imagine-this): Scenario-based storytelling — three mission contexts showing daily life with CROS.

## Lexicon — CROS Vocabulary

CROS defines its own civic ontology — terms like "relationship memory," "narrative intelligence," and "communal stewardship."

- [Lexicon Index](https://thecros.app/lexicon): All defined terms in the CROS vocabulary with schema.org DefinedTerm markup.

## Library — Knowledge Hub

- [Library Index](https://thecros.app/library): Calm discovery space connecting roles, archetypes, philosophy, and concepts.

## Field Journal & Insights

- [Field Journal](https://thecros.app/field-journal): Role-based narrative entries from the perspective of Shepherds, Companions, Visitors, and Stewards.
- [Insights](https://thecros.app/insights): Thought leadership — deeper explorations of relationship-centered work and community technology.
- [Why Traditional CRMs Fail Nonprofits](https://thecros.app/insights/why-crm-fails-nonprofits): Sales pipelines were never designed for community work.
- [Relationship Memory Matters](https://thecros.app/insights/relationship-memory-matters): Why organizations forget the people they serve.
- [Essays](https://thecros.app/essays): Living essay library — long-form pieces generated from platform signals.
- [Reflections](https://thecros.app/reflections): Monthly contemplative content connecting platform movement to narrative insight.

## Mission Atlas

Geographic narrative surfaces showing how mission work happens across different metro types and organization archetypes.

- [Mission Atlas Index](https://thecros.app/mission-atlas): Browsable map connecting archetypes to metro types.

## Network & Public Presence

- [Network Directory](https://thecros.app/network): Public communio directory — organizations using CROS, filtered by archetype and metro.
- [Authority Hub](https://thecros.app/authority): Trust signals aggregating all content that establishes CROS as a credible voice in community technology.

## Legal

- [Terms of Service](https://thecros.app/legal/terms): Plain-language legal framework for CROS usage.
- [Privacy Policy](https://thecros.app/legal/privacy): Data handling, retention, and user rights.
- [Data Processing Agreement](https://thecros.app/legal/data-processing): GDPR-aligned DPA for organizations handling sensitive community data.
- [Acceptable Use](https://thecros.app/legal/acceptable-use): Usage boundaries aligned with CROS values of dignity and privacy.
- [AI Transparency](https://thecros.app/legal/ai-transparency): How NRI uses AI — transparency about what AI does and doesn't do in CROS.

## Key Concepts

- **Relationship Memory**: The institutional knowledge that lives in human connections — visits, reflections, conversations, and shared experiences. CROS preserves this memory so it doesn't leave when staff does.
- **NRI (Narrative Relational Intelligence)**: Human-first intelligence built from reflections, events attended, conversations, and community signals. AI assists the system, but the intelligence belongs to the human relationships.
- **Communio**: An opt-in inter-organization collaboration network where anonymized signals flow between CROS tenants, creating collective awareness without exposing private data.
- **Signum**: The signal intelligence layer that surfaces community events, partner movements, and local pulse — so organizations notice what matters without manual searching.
- **Testimonium**: The narrative storytelling layer that transforms daily field observations into board-ready impact stories and anonymized public signals.
- **Impulsus**: A private, sacred journal for leaders — observations that never feed metrics or reports.
- **Drift Detection**: Gentle awareness of relationships that may need attention — no alarms, just quiet noticing.
- **Journey Chapters**: Instead of CRM pipeline stages, CROS uses narrative chapters that honor the complexity of human relationships.

## About

CROS was born from Profunda — a relationship memory system built for Regional Impact Managers in nonprofit ecosystems. It was never a sales CRM. It focused on relationship memory, community awareness, and narrative intelligence. Profunda is now the founding internal instance that inspired CROS.

CROS stands for Communal Relationship Operating System. For 40 years, technology has been driven by Operating Systems and Artificial Intelligence — both invented by humans, but both largely remained cold, data-driven, and impersonal. CROS replaces the OS with a human touch. It is the bridge between an organization and the community it serves.

Website: https://thecros.app
Contact: https://thecros.app/contact
